_meta.purpose: FEAT-MEM-05 parity eval — fleet-memory (vector) vs Graphiti (FalkorDB graph) on guardkit's real read domains
_meta.scope_note: Queries target the HARVESTED-knowledge domain (architecture/decisions/patterns/process) — the coach-context + feature-plan reads FEAT-MEM-08 cutover must preserve. Runtime-captured knowledge (task_outcomes, failure_patterns) is intentionally OUT of scope: fleet-memory acquires it post-cutover via /task-complete writes, so it would be an expected (not a parity) gap.
_meta.graphiti_search: guardkit graphiti search "<query>" -n 5  (broad, all categories — matches fleet-memory searching its whole guardkit corpus)

probes.query: What are the quality gate phases?
What is the Player-Coach pattern in autobuild?
When should I use the SDK versus a subprocess?
What is the walking skeleton approach?
How does adversarial cooperation work between the player and coach?
How is implementation complexity evaluated and routed to a review mode?
What is the BDD Gherkin scenario approach for feature specs?
How are typed payloads and deterministic content-hash upsert designed?
How does the NATS relay handle poison episodes and the dead letter queue?
What is the retrieval API and token-budgeted context assembly design?
How does the Graphiti to fleet-memory migration and cutover work?
What is the local-first inference decision for the dark factory?
What are the conventions for task files, feature planning and waves?
How does the coach validate player output against quality gates?
What is the false-green problem in autobuild and how is it prevented?
How is per-task verification enforced before a task is marked complete?
